worker_task: Create a new alpha factor in the AlphaForge framework that uses a volume-weighted momentum signal. Write the factor to a file named 'vwap_momentum_factor.py' in the factors directory. The factor should: 1) load daily price and volume data from a data source (assume a CSV with columns: date, ticker, close, volume, vwap), 2) compute the 20-day rolling VWAP, 3) calculate the percentage difference between current close and 20-day VWAP, 4) normalize the signal to z-scores over a 60-day rolling window, 5) generate long/short signals based on z-score thresholds of ±1.5. Train on data from 2020-01-01 to 2021-12-31, test on 2022-01-01 to 2022-12-31. Run a parameter sweep over lookback periods [10, 20, 30, 40] and z-score thresholds [1.0, 1.5, 2.0] (12 combinations total). Output a CSV with columns: ticker, date, signal, and a performance summary. In your completion summary, include: train_start, train_end, test_start, test_end, n_combinations (12), and the metric (IC or Sharpe ratio of the best parameter set).
rationale: Start with a simple, well-understood momentum factor that combines price and volume information. VWAP-based momentum is a common alpha signal that can be easily implemented and tested. The parameter sweep is small (12 combos) to keep the iteration safe and fast.
success_criteria: Factor file is syntactically valid Python
CSV output has correct columns (ticker, date, signal)
Parameter sweep runs all 12 combinations without errors
Completion summary includes all required report fields
risk_notes: Ensure the factor does not modify any protected files (evaluation.py, factors.py, simulation_adapter.py, fast_simulator.py, authority_map.md). The data loading path should be parameterized or read from a config to avoid hardcoded paths. Volume data may have missing values; handle with forward fill or dropna.
